title: My Body Is Not a Project to Finish
subtitle: An affirmation for when the body feels like a problem to solve
excerpt: A short affirmation for when the body has become a project to complete rather than a home to live in. The statement sets a different frame. Useful in the morning before the mental to-do list about the body gets its grip.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 2 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use in the morning when the mental project-list about your body is already running before you have got out of bed.

content.text: A one-minute affirmation for the mornings when the body feels like an unfinished project. The project framing keeps the body at arm's length: something to be worked on, not something to live in. This statement offers a different starting point.
New to affirmations as a practice? Read
The project-body frame is useful for getting things done. It is not useful as an operating mode from the moment you wake up. The affirmation is a one-sentence redirect: from project to home.
My body is not a project to finish. It is the place I live.

Say it out loud three times in the morning, hand on your sternum. Say it before the project-list starts. Say it on the days when you feel behind with the body work, and especially on the days when you feel ahead.
Adapted from Day 2 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
